subject: Project Management
title: The Sprint
description: A quick win: understand The Sprint.

hook.text: A sprint is a fixed-length cycle — usually two weeks — where the team builds a usable increment.

buildup.text: No new work is added mid-sprint. The scope is locked so the team can focus.

discovery.text: At the end, something working exists. Not perfect, not finished, but real and demonstrable.

twist.text: The time box creates urgency. Teams learn to cut scope rather than extend time.

climax.text: Sprints turn projects from marathons into a series of predictable, winnable races.

punchline.text: Two weeks. One goal. Ship something real.

quiz.question: What happens at the end of every sprint?
options: A usable product increment is delivered
The team takes a week off
